_comment: Per-project IDENTITY for the generalized owner/operator harness — Stock-Track's OWN instance. Holds NAMES/PATHS/IDS only, NEVER secret VALUES. This is a re-instantiation of the Blueprint-Fitness harness PATTERN with Stock-Track's OWN Firebase project (easy-stock-track). Contains ZERO Blueprint identifiers by design (proven by harness/harness_antileak_scan.sh). Secrets are never here: the orchestrator authenticates via ADC (gcloud application-default), never a committed key. ${a.b} interpolates another key. Safe to commit.

firebase._comment: serviceAccountPath is a PATH REFERENCE only — NO key file is ever read at runtime. The orchestrator authenticates via ADC (gcloud application-default), never a committed key; service-account*.json is gitignored. The field only satisfies the schema and documents where a key WOULD live if one were ever used — it is intentionally inert in the no-key story.

owner._comment: Anonymous Auth means the owner UID is a RUNTIME value (per install), NOT a fixed literal. This placeholder only satisfies the schema/interpolation; the app keys the thread by the live anon uid, and stocktrack_chat.js AUTO-DISCOVERS the active thread (or takes --uid / STOCKTRACK_OWNER_UID).

push._comment: Push-notification parity (ported Blueprint pattern). title/androidChannelId/dataRoute drive the app + orchestrator-side FCM send. tokenCollection/tokenField say WHERE the app stores the device FCM token (config-driven, kept inside the harness's OWN chat collection so no extra collection is introduced) and WHERE the orchestrator reads it. The orchestrator sends via admin.messaging().send (ADC, no key).
push.title: Stock-Track Ops

harness._comment: In-app HONESTY signals for the owner harness. orchestratorBridge DECLARES whether a real operator loop is reading THIS project's chat/poke: 'off' = nobody is reading yet (writes still land in the backend), 'live' = an operator is polling. Drives the mode banner + poke/send honesty so a functional-looking-but-disconnected channel can never mislead a dogfooder. backendLabel is a generic human label for the connected backend (interpolated, never a hardcoded app noun). Flip orchestratorBridge to 'live' + set backendLabel only when the operator loop is actually running (owner A/B decision — see the connectivity spec).
